frameworks.name: task-optimized deep neural network
frameworks.one_line: DNN trained to classify images and predict its own correctness models confidence

frameworks.name: balance-of-evidence rule
frameworks.one_line: optimal account of confidence using full evidence distribution

frameworks.name: response-congruent-evidence heuristic
frameworks.one_line: heuristic confidence read-out using only evidence congruent with the choice

frameworks.name: metacognition / decision confidence
frameworks.one_line: metacognitive assessment of decision correctness as the target phenomenon

core_question: Can a task-optimized neural network reproduce the behavioral dissociations between decisions and confidence?
key_assumptions: confidence is the model's predicted probability of being correct
optimization for naturalistic image statistics yields humanlike read-outs
decisions and confidence can share computations within one network
manipulations: joint training to classify images and predict own correctness
comparison of learned strategy against balance-of-evidence and response-congruent rules
key_findings: the trained network naturally reproduces key decision-confidence dissociations
neither balance-of-evidence nor response-congruent-evidence fully describes the learned strategy
the model approximates the distribution of its training data flexibly
interpretations: apparent suboptimality in human confidence may reflect optimization for naturalistic statistics
confidence read-out is a learned, data-distribution-shaped function, not a fixed rule
